poverty_guidelines_annual.note: Annual federal poverty guidelines (HHS, illustrative figures). Verify current year figures against official HHS publication before relying on this for real decisions.

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (Food Stamps)
programs.plain_language_summary: Helps cover grocery costs for low-income individuals and families through a monthly benefit card.

criteria.label: Income limit
criteria.description: Gross monthly household income at or below 130% of the federal poverty line for your household size

criteria.label: Residency
criteria.description: Must live in the state where you are applying

criteria.label: Work requirement (ABAWD rule)
criteria.description: Able-bodied adults aged 18-49 without dependents may need to meet work or training-hour requirements unless exempt (disability, caregiving, pregnancy, etc.)

required_documents: Proof of income (recent pay stubs or benefit letters)
Proof of residency (utility bill, lease, or mail with your address)
Proof of household composition (e.g., birth certificates for children, if applicable)
next_steps: Apply online through your state's benefits portal or visit a local office
A caseworker will review your application and confirm your eligibility and benefit amount

programs.name: Medicaid
programs.plain_language_summary: Provides free or low-cost health coverage for eligible low-income individuals and families.

criteria.label: Income limit (children)
criteria.description: Children under 19 in households with income up to a higher threshold than adults (commonly around 200% of the federal poverty line, but exact percentage varies by state)

criteria.label: Income limit (adults without dependents)
criteria.description: In states that have not expanded Medicaid, coverage for adults without dependents is very limited and depends on specific categories (pregnant, disabled, elderly, caretaker, etc.). Texas follows strict income rules - a caseworker must confirm your specific category.

criteria.label: Residency & citizenship/immigration status
criteria.description: Must live in the state where applying and be a U.S. citizen or fall into a qualified immigration category. Specific immigration-status rules are complex and vary by category and state law.

required_documents: Proof of citizenship or immigration status
next_steps: Apply through your state Medicaid portal or call 2-1-1 for local help
A caseworker will determine which specific Medicaid eligibility category applies to you

programs.name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program
programs.plain_language_summary: Helps eligible households pay heating and cooling bills, and in some cases covers emergency repairs or weatherization.

criteria.label: Income limit
criteria.description: Household income at or below roughly 150% of the federal poverty line (exact percentage and method can vary by state)

criteria.label: Residency & energy cost responsibility
criteria.description: Must live in the state and be responsible for home energy costs, either directly or included in rent

criteria.label: Priority status (not a qualifying/disqualifying criterion)
criteria.description: Households with elderly members, young children, or persons with disabilities may receive priority processing - this affects how fast you're helped, not whether you qualify.

required_documents: Photo ID for all adult household members
next_steps: Contact your local Community Action Agency for LIHEAP applications
Apply as early as possible - funding is limited and processed seasonally
programs.source_name: Administration for Children and Families - LIHEAP
